source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
<image1>
Albert places these 5 figures <image2>, <image3>, <image4>, <image5>, <image6> on a 5x5-grid. Each figure is only allowed to appear once in every column and in every row. Which figure does Albert have to place on the field with the question mark?
<image7>
extraction_notes: options field only contains labels A-E; no textual option content is provided outside the image

image_dependency: question_text explicitly references multiple embedded images for the figures and the grid ('<image1>' ... '<image7>')
the missing figure cannot be determined from text alone because the actual grid placements and figure appearances are visual
multi_step: the task requires combining visual grid state with the row-and-column uniqueness constraint
this is a Latin-square-style deduction problem: infer which figure fits the question-mark cell after checking existing figures across its row and column
there are 5 candidate options, so solving is not a direct lookup and likely involves at least two constraint-checking steps
verifiability: answer_type is choice/option and answer_text is a single option ('A'), which is easy to compare against a gold answer
the puzzle rule is explicit: each figure appears only once per row and column, so a proposed answer can be checked against the visual grid
asset registry passed, image exists, and image readability_score is high (0.8859), supporting downstream verification

risk_flags: answer_text is only an option label, not the figure identity itself
visual grid content is not serialized in text, so verification depends on image access

decision_reasons: Core assets are present and registry passed: question, image, and answer are all available with no recorded issues.
Although the problem is highly image-dependent, the required image exists, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring indicates strong verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value.
The answer is only an option label, but this is acceptable for a multiple-choice item when the image and question context are intact.
